message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first timeouts in distributed handshake + accept (tick 287)

4 ambient `runtime_compat::timeout` call sites in main.rs distributed
agent paths migrated to `timeout_with_cx`:

**Server-side handshake + message loop (aggregator):**

- L13068 handshake-line read: `timeout_with_cx(&handshake_cx,
  DISTRIBUTED_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT, distributed_read_line(...))` — reads
  the agent's initial hello handshake.
- L13191 per-message read (reuses `handshake_cx`): wraps each subsequent
  `distributed_read_line` in a 30s timeout. A cx-cancel during a slow
  agent now aborts the read cleanly instead of running out the timeout.

**Listener accept loop (aggregator):**

- L13391 accept timeout: `timeout_with_cx(&accept_cx,
  Duration::from_millis(500), listener.accept())` — wraps each
  `TcpListener::accept()` in a 500ms timeout so the loop can check
  `shutdown_flag` between accepts.

**Client-side handshake response (agent):**

- L14130 handshake-response read: `timeout_with_cx(&agent_handshake_cx,
  DISTRIBUTED_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT, distributed_read_line(...))` — reads
  the aggregator's handshake ack after the agent sends its hello.

All four sites use `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` with
shared bindings where possible (handshake_cx reused for the per-message
loop, accept_cx reused across loop iterations).

The distributed agent/aggregator TLS protocol has multiple nested
timeouts — handshake (30s), per-message (30s), accept (500ms). Every
timeout now respects cx-cancel, so an operator-cancellable Cx at the
outer session task cleanly unwinds the full TCP/TLS stack instead of
blocking on pending reads.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 101 (187-287).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 185 sites (181 before + 4).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
